metadata.title: Guideline on Similar Biological Medicinal Products Containing Monoclonal Antibodies – Non-clinical and Clinical Issues
metadata.notes: No prior extraction found in output-v2, v3, or v4. Full extraction performed.

rules.rule_text: Non-clinical studies must be performed before initiating clinical trials; in vitro studies must be conducted first, followed by a determination of whether in vivo work is warranted.
rules.verbatim_quote: Non-clinical studies should be performed before initiating clinical trials. In vitro studies should be conducted first and a decision then made as to the extent of what, if any, in vivo work will be required.
rules.exceptions: None stated

rules.rule_text: In vitro non-clinical studies must include assays on: binding to target antigen(s), binding to representative isoforms of the three Fc gamma receptors (FcγRI, FcγRII, FcγRIII), FcRn and complement (C1q), Fab-associated functions, and Fc-associated functions (ADCC, CDC, complement activation).
rules.verbatim_quote: In vitro non-clinical studies should be performed with an appropriate number of batches of product representative of that intended to be used in the clinical trial. These studies should include relevant assays on: Binding to target antigen(s); Binding to representative isoforms of the relevant three Fc gamma receptors (FcγRI, FcγRII and FcγRIII), FcRn and complement (C1q); Fab-associated functions (e.g. neutralization of a soluble ligand, receptor activation or blockade); Fc-associated functions (e.g. antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity, ADCC; complement-dependent cytotoxicity, CDC; complement activation)
rules.exceptions: Evaluation of ADCC and CDC is generally not needed for mAbs directed against non-membrane bound targets. Tissue cross-reactivity studies are not recommended for assessing comparability.

rules.rule_text: In vitro studies must be comparative in nature and designed to be sensitive enough to detect differences in the concentration-activity relationship, not just assess response per se.
rules.verbatim_quote: These studies should be comparative in nature and should be designed to be sensitive enough to detect differences in the concentration–activity relationship between the similar biological medicinal product and the reference medicinal product, and should not just assess the response per se.
rules.exceptions: None stated

rules.rule_text: Before determining the need for in vivo studies, factors must be evaluated including: presence of relevant quality attributes not detected in the reference product, quality attributes in significantly different amounts, and relevant differences in formulation.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2 - Determination of the need for in vivo studies = step 2
rules.verbatim_quote: Factors to be considered when the need for additional in vivo non-clinical studies is evaluated, include but are not restricted to: Presence of relevant quality attributes that have not been detected in the reference product (e.g new post-translational modification structure); Presence of quality attributes in significantly different amounts than those measured in the reference product; Relevant differences in formulation, e.g. use of excipients not widely used for mAbs.
rules.exceptions: Each factor alone does not necessarily warrant in vivo testing; factors must be considered together

rules.rule_text: Repeated dose toxicity studies in non-human primates are usually not recommended for biosimilar mAbs.
rules.verbatim_quote: The conduct of repeated dose toxicity studies in non-human primates is usually not recommended.
rules.exceptions: If an in vivo study is deemed necessary, the focus depends on the need for additional information and availability of a relevant animal model

rules.rule_text: Safety pharmacology and reproduction toxicology are not required for non-clinical testing of biosimilar mAbs.
rules.verbatim_quote: Studies regarding safety pharmacology and reproduction toxicology are not required for non-clinical testing of biosimilar mAbs.
rules.exceptions: Local tolerance may need to be evaluated if excipients are introduced for which there is no or little experience with the intended clinical route

rules.rule_text: Comparative clinical studies between the biosimilar and reference medicinal product must always be conducted; the number and type of studies must be justified based on a sound scientific rationale using a stepwise approach.
rules.verbatim_quote: Comparative clinical studies between the biosimilar and reference medicinal product should always be conducted. The number and type of studies might vary according to the reference product and should be justified based on a sound scientific rationale. A stepwise approach is normally recommended throughout the development programme.
rules.exceptions: None stated

rules.rule_text: A comparative pharmacokinetic study in a sufficiently sensitive and homogeneous study population (healthy volunteers or patients) normally forms the initial step of biosimilar mAb clinical development.
rules.verbatim_quote: The comparison of the pharmacokinetic properties of the biosimilar product and the reference medicinal product forms normally the first step of a biosimilar mAb development.
rules.exceptions: A study in healthy volunteers may not be possible in case of toxic mechanism of action; a study in patients may be a better option

rules.rule_text: Bioanalytical assays for PK studies must be appropriate for their intended use and adequately validated per the Guideline on bioanalytical method validation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Furthermore, bioanalytical assays should be appropriate for their intended use and adequately validated as outlined in the 'Guideline on bioanalytical method validation' (EMEA/CHMP/EWP/192217/2009).
rules.exceptions: None stated

rules.rule_text: In a single dose PK study, the primary parameter must be AUC(0-inf); Cmax, tmax, volume of distribution, and half-life should also be estimated as secondary parameters.
rules.verbatim_quote: In a single dose study, the primary parameter should be the AUC(0-inf). Secondary parameters such as Cmax, tmax, volume of distribution, and half-life, should also be estimated. In case of subcutaneous administration, Cmax should be a co-primary parameter.
rules.exceptions: In subcutaneous administration, Cmax is a co-primary parameter; if no IV data provided, partial AUCs should be assessed

rules.rule_text: PK comparability margins must be defined a priori and appropriately justified; any widening beyond the conventional 80-125% equivalence margin for primary parameters requires thorough justification including estimation of potential impact on clinical efficacy and safety.
rules.verbatim_quote: Comparability margins have to be defined a priori and appropriately justified... any widening of the conventional equivalence margin beyond 80-125% for the primary parameters requires thorough justification, including an estimation of potential impact on clinical efficacy and safety.
rules.exceptions: For secondary parameters, confidence intervals for ratio or differences can be presented with descriptive statistics without acceptance ranges

rules.rule_text: Anti-drug antibodies must be measured in parallel with PK assessment using the most appropriate sampling time points.
rules.verbatim_quote: Anti-drug antibodies should be measured in parallel of PK assessment using the most appropriate sampling time points.
rules.exceptions: None stated

rules.rule_text: For PD markers to constitute pivotal evidence of efficacy comparability, two prerequisites must be met: a clear dose-response relationship must be shown, and at least one PD marker must be an accepted surrogate that can be related to patient outcome.
rules.source_section: Section 5.2.2 - PD markers as pivotal proof of comparability
rules.verbatim_quote: The following prerequisites need to be met to accept that PD markers can constitute the pivotal evidence for the efficacy comparability exercise: A clear dose-response relationship is shown. At least one PD marker is an accepted surrogate marker and can be related to patient outcome to the extent that demonstration of similar effect on the PD marker will ensure a similar effect on the clinical outcome variable.
rules.exceptions: If these prerequisites are not met, clinical efficacy trials (Step 2) must be conducted

rules.rule_text: Similar clinical efficacy between the biosimilar and reference product must be demonstrated in adequately powered, randomised, parallel group comparative clinical trials, preferably double-blind and normally equivalence trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: reference product should be demonstrated in adequately powered, randomised, parallel group comparative clinical trial(s), preferably double-blind, normally equivalence trials.
rules.exceptions: Deviations from CHMP disease-specific guidelines may be warranted in some circumstances but must be scientifically justified

rules.rule_text: The guiding principle for clinical comparability is to demonstrate similar clinical efficacy and safety compared to the reference medicinal product, not patient benefit per se.
rules.verbatim_quote: The guiding principle is to demonstrate similar clinical efficacy and safety compared to the reference medicinal product, not patient benefit per se, which has already been shown for the reference medicinal product.
rules.exceptions: None stated

rules.rule_text: Systematic and comparative evaluation of immunogenicity is required throughout the clinical development programme; immunogenicity assessment must use the same validated assay(s) for both the biosimilar and reference mAb.
rules.verbatim_quote: Systematic and comparative evaluation and discussion of immunogenicity is important, due to clinical consequences like loss of efficacy and also likely resistance against further treatment with the reference mAb... Comparative assessment of unwanted immune responses against the biosimilar and the reference mAb are normally undertaken as part of the clinical study establishing similar clinical efficacy and safety, using the same validated assay(s).
rules.exceptions: None stated

rules.rule_text: Extrapolation of clinical efficacy and safety data to other indications of the reference mAb is possible based on overall evidence from the comparability exercise with adequate justification; when different mechanisms of action are relevant, additional data must be provided.
rules.verbatim_quote: Extrapolation of clinical efficacy and safety data to other indications of the reference mAb, not specifically studied during the clinical development of the biosimilar mAb, is possible based on the overall evidence of comparability provided from the comparability exercise and with adequate justification. If pivotal evidence for comparability is based on PD and for the claimed indications different mechanisms of action are relevant (or uncertainty exists), then applicants should provide relevant data to support extrapolation to all claimed clinical indications.
rules.exceptions: Extrapolation requires comprehensive discussion of available literature including the involved antigen receptor(s) and mechanism(s) of action

rules.rule_text: A risk management plan/pharmacovigilance plan must be presented at the time of marketing authorisation application in accordance with current EU legislation and pharmacovigilance guidelines.
rules.verbatim_quote: For the marketing authorisation procedure the applicant should present a risk management plan/pharmacovigilance plan in accordance with current EU legislation and pharmacovigilance guidelines. Risk minimisation activities in place for the reference medicinal product may have to be implemented as well into the Risk Management Plan of the biosimilar.
rules.exceptions: None stated

rules.rule_text: All appropriate measures must be taken to clearly identify any biological medicinal product which is the subject of a suspected adverse reaction report, with due regard to the name of the medicinal product and the batch number.
rules.verbatim_quote: Therefore, all appropriate measures should be taken to identify clearly any biological medicinal product which is the subject of a suspected adverse reaction report, with due regard to the name of the medicinal product and the batch number.
rules.exceptions: None stated

rules.rule_text: When PD markers are planned as pivotal evidence for biosimilarity, the approach must be discussed with regulatory authorities in advance, including a proposal for the size of the equivalence margin and its clinical justification.
rules.source_section: Section 5.2.2 - PD markers as pivotal proof of comparability
rules.verbatim_quote: When PD markers are planned as pivotal evidence to establish similarity, it is recommended to discuss such approach with regulatory authorities. This should include a proposal of the size of the proposed equivalence margin and its clinical justification as regards lack of a clinical meaningful difference.
rules.exceptions: None stated
